metadata.title:
PUBLIC GETS A
W.W.P, OBJECT


LESSON

pages.raw_text:
The incompetence of one of the
new men whom the Washington
Water Power company is breaking
in to take the place of the en-
ployes who have joined the union.
yesterday resulted seriously for
John Seigfreid, an employe OF the
Diamond Ice & Fuel company.
-. Seigfreid was driving an ice
wagon, being the owner of his own
horses, and had just come to the
corner Of Sharp avenue and Hamil.
ton street and was crossing the
tracks when q street car Of the
Washington Water Power COM
pany, coming at a speed such as
the company always runs its Hill.
yard cars, swooped down upon the
team. The driven was thrown into
the air and alighted upon the
fender, while his team was knock
ed down and one Of the horses
caught. The animal was torn
loose from its harness and with
Seigfreid on top of the fender and
his squeezing horse underneath, the
car was carried by Its speed half q
block before the incompetent motor
man could bring 1t to a stop.


The motorman wore no uniform
and no company cap. He could
not be identified. Seigfreid was so
badly scared he failed to get the
number Of the car.


When the car finally stopped,
people rushed out and assisted him
to his feet and pulled the dying
horse from under the car. The
horse was shot to put it out of
misery.


While Seigfreid, who was prac-
tically uninjured, was still I'M
steady on his feet, the conductor
rushed up to him with a yellow
slip Of paper in his hand and
Seigfreid was just able to read
where it certified that the COM
pany was blameless." He refused
to sign the paper.


There have been numerous acct
dents in the vicinity of streets
traversed by these swift North
Center cars. They gain a speed
second to a railroad train and
make no stops between the center
Of town and south Of Center street.
No stops are made at street cross
ms.


Secretary Blocker Of the Wash.
ington Water Power company to-
day denied any knowledge eof an
accident having happened.
